title: Deep re-extraction (multi-sample consensus) for high-fidelity diagram rebuild — shipped as gate-and-dock

oracle.note: Oracle = FD-338 Jira acceptance criteria, accepted by Patrick in-session; UI criteria C6-C12 derived from the built rebuild flow.

criteria.text: A rebuild reliably reconnects the canonical fragmented family to >=90% connectivity
criteria.evidence: Re-proved in GT learning loop round (2026-06-10): 2 consecutive full real-LLM rebuilds of the canonical diagram scored 95.0% and 97.5% LCC (GT-scorer projection, post-dock). Prior battery: 7/7 production-pipeline runs 94.3-97.3% keep-User LCC (2026-06-09).

criteria.text: Per-discussion extraction quality (F1) does not regress
criteria.evidence: Re-proved after the GT-loop dock-prompt change (3-run confirmation 2026-06-10): mean vs 2026-05-21 baseline aggregate -0.005, people -0.005, events -0.010, pair_bonds -0.016, parent_child -0.003 — all within the +/-0.05 band; People/Events gates pass. Prior fix-round result: aggregate -0.013, people +0.003, events -0.042, pair_bonds -0.009. OVERNIGHT 2026-06-24 (FD-321 naming ON, 3 runs): People F1 0.917-0.936 (gate >0.70 PASS), Events F1 0.395-0.457 (gate >0.30 PASS), PairBonds ~0.82-0.84, ParentChild ~0.83-0.84. FD-321's proband-name-into-extraction change does not regress per-discussion quality.

criteria.text: Rebuild runs async with live progress and cooperative cancel through the real task queue
criteria.evidence: Real Celery smoke on sandbox (solo pool, isolated broker): task completed in 121s with PROGRESS states ('Finding missing people...', 'Reconnecting separated family members...'); orphan watchdog verified (un-polled task self-cancelled at TTL).

criteria.text: Merge and dock logic covered by mocked-LLM tests
criteria.evidence: btcopilot suite 796 passed (incl. 9 dock tests, weld/majority-vote/K=1 tests); familydiagram personal suite 176 passed.

criteria.text: Cost per rebuild stays in the measured range
criteria.evidence: ~$0.11 per K=1 rebuild measured across the acceptance battery (vs $0.23 K=4 budget in the ticket).

criteria.text: Rebuild action is visible and enabled on a diagram with a discussion and people
criteria.evidence: Real Personal app (worktree code, bridge on 9899) against sandbox 8889: with 0 people rebuildButton visible=false; after UI extract+Accept All committed 7 people (diagram 2011 v4), rebuildButton visible=true enabled=true with discussion 61 selected.

criteria.text: Rebuild shows a live progress overlay in the real app UI and completes
criteria.evidence: Overlay transitions watched live via bridge: visible=true progress=0 'Starting rebuild...' cancellable=true -> progress=10 'Finding missing people and connections… (pass 1 of 8)' -> closed on completion; both runs were real Celery tasks on sandbox worker (b20d9173 k=1 succeeded 15.6s, a233eb57 k=8 succeeded 107s in sandbox_celery.log), driven by real HTTP enqueue+1s status polls in sandbox_flask.log.

criteria.text: Completed rebuild stages its results in the review sheet; no junk 'Update -> null' card; badge count matches visible cards
criteria.evidence: After real k=1 rebuild on diagram 2011: sheet auto-opened, cardStack count=6 = itemCount=6 = badge pdpCount=6; staged delta had 8 rows and the 2 excluded were exactly the name-null parents-only edit rows (committed ids 10,11) that would render 'Update -> null'; all 6 rendered cards (4 named people + 2 bonds) resolve non-null names.

criteria.text: Accept All applies the staged rebuild to the diagram, including relationship edits on already-committed people
criteria.evidence: Clicked acceptAllButton in the real sheet: scene 7 -> 11 people; dev DB diagram 2011 v8 shows already-committed Carol Hughes (id 11, parents was null) now parents=30 = new Evelyn(33)+Frank(34) bond, 4 new people + 2 new bonds committed, server PDP drained to [].

criteria.text: Applied rebuild persists across full app relaunch
criteria.evidence: App process SIGTERMed (verified dead), relaunched with same sandbox: fresh process re-fetched diagram 2011 (v10) over HTTP and the reloaded scene shows 11 Person / 4 Marriage / 7 ChildOf — exactly the committed DB structure incl. Carol's new parents bond; asserted from the rebuilt scene, not UI memory.

criteria.text: Max-fidelity toggle off sends k=1, on sends k=8
criteria.evidence: Server-side parse of the real HTTP bodies in sandbox_flask.log: toggle OFF -> 'started deep_reextract task b20d9173... k=1'; rebuildMaxFidelitySwitch clicked ON (checked=true read back) -> 'started deep_reextract task a233eb57... k=8'; celery task results echo k=1/k=8 respectively.

criteria.text: The rebuilt family is structurally correct per the user's own knowledge of their family, judged on their real diagram
Ex-partner (romantic, never married) attaches to the proband as a pair-bond staged with married=false so it renders dashed
criteria.evidence: GT scorer round 2026-06-10: final full rebuild staged the ex-partner bond to the proband with married=false (both ex-partner assertions present); isolated dock probe 3/3 accepted with contiguous verbatim quotes, friend singleton stayed unattached 3/3 (zero false attaches). Plumbing: optional married flag on staged PairBond, commit mapping coerces None->married (legacy solid), dock applier sets false for romantic-not-married.

criteria.text: Owner GT edge assertions (5 corrections from the human walk) hold across 2 consecutive full rebuilds
criteria.evidence: Loop outcome 2026-06-10: best rebuild 5/7 required present, 5 forbidden violated, LCC 97.5 — not clean. The 5 remaining failures are deterministic committed-data poison on the canonical diagram (identical across 2 consecutive rebuilds); the additive delta cannot remove committed wrong-parent links or spurious bonds. Unblock requires Patrick's call: one-time supervised repair of the committed data vs a committed-correction feature (delete/committed-edit channels exist in schema but nothing populates them). Diagnosis: /tmp/fd338_evidence/ralph/iter2_diagnosis.md. OVERNIGHT 2026-06-24 (GT extended to 32 assertions = 5 first-walk + 8 second-walk N1-N8): scored on a NAMED FROM-EMPTY PROXY (single accumulation + dock, no committed base, no merge_runs consensus). 5 runs: required 5-7/17, forbidden clean 12-15/15, LCC 70-96.7%. FD-321's own correction CLEARS deterministically (proband duplicate gone, no 'Client', 5/5). Always-failing required (5/5): proband parents wrong (R1), brother absent (R5), wife is nobody's parent (N2), brother's-wife bond absent (N5a), trio's named mother never created (N7a/b/c), nephew on wrong uncle (N8a). Mechanism class = merge weld / missing consensus + placeholder fabrication; NOT fixable by a bounded downstream edit (prior placeholder-spouse reconciliation pass measured, no effect). CAVEAT: the proxy omits merge_runs against the committed base and understates structural quality; the honest C12/C14 verdict needs the real deep_reextract entrypoint (K=1, committed base = named primary on 1924). Report: /tmp/fd338_evidence/overnight/OVERNIGHT_REPORT.md and btcopilot-sources/training/gt-exports/OVERNIGHT_REPORT_2026-06-24.md.

findings.summary: Extraction stages a duplicate person named 'Client' bridged to committed people (7/7 acceptance runs) ROOT-CAUSE FIX TRACKED: FD-321 (user name + birth date on the primary node, fed into extraction context) — re-parented to FD-264 2026-06-11.
findings.repro: Any K=1 rebuild of diagram 1924 discussions 55,58,60; inspect staged delta people

findings.summary: Per-item review ending on a reject never advances the re-extraction cursor (pre-existing FD-333 gap)
findings.repro: Accept all staged cards one-by-one, reject the last one; cursor never advances

findings.summary: Merge cross-matched a new sibling to a committed person sharing a last name: k=1 rebuild of seeded discussion 61 matched extracted 'Ben Hughes' onto committed 'Dennis Hughes' (id 10), so Ben vanished from the delta, a duplicate 'Dennis Hughes' was staged as new, and committed Dennis got a parents edit making him a child of Carol + his own duplicate; the follow-up k=8 run staged a duplicate 'Carol Hughes' over committed Carol the same way — same dup-over-committed class as deferred F-001
findings.repro: Seed user fd338-uitest@example.com diagram 2011 discussion 61 (scripts in /tmp/fd338_evidence/agent/), extract+accept statements 1-5, add statements 6-9, run rebuild; inspect staged delta

findings.summary: After Accept All the app's local diagram copy retains the drained parents-edit rows in its pdp while the server pdp is empty — user-invisible (badge and sheet both 0) and self-corrects on reload, but local state diverges from server until then
findings.repro: Accept All a rebuild delta containing parents-edit rows; compare get_personal_state(pdp) personCount (2) vs server diagram pdp ([])

findings.summary: objectName 'rebuildOverlayCancelButton' exists in both LoadingOverlay instances (extractOverlay and importOverlay); the bridge element finder resolves the hidden importOverlay copy, so the live rebuild Cancel button cannot be driven by the harness (clicked it: no-op)
findings.repro: During a rebuild, bridge click rebuildOverlayCancelButton; get_property shows visible=false (importOverlay's) while extractOverlay.cancellable=true

findings.summary: Rebuild button is hidden until a discussion is selected — no affordance tells the user why; found by Patrick mid-walk (button appeared after selecting a discussion via the Discuss title dropdown)
findings.repro: Launch Personal app fresh, open a populated diagram, stay on Discuss tab without selecting a discussion; rebuild button absent

findings.summary: Rebuilt diagram: proband attached to the wrong parent couple (cross-matched among three same-first-name people; generation flip). GT detail: btcopilot-sources/training/gt-exports/diagram-1924-structure-gt.md (confidential, outside repo)
findings.repro: Rebuild diagram 1924 (K=1) via the app; compare drawn structure to owner GT

findings.summary: Rebuilt diagram: a person's mother and son-in-law paired as a couple (wrong pair-bond synthesized as co-parents). GT detail: btcopilot-sources/training/gt-exports/diagram-1924-structure-gt.md (confidential, outside repo)
findings.repro: Rebuild diagram 1924 (K=1) via the app; compare drawn structure to owner GT

findings.summary: Dock applier: sibling_of with placeholder parents attaches only the floating member, not the anchor — anchor's own parents link never set. GT detail: btcopilot-sources/training/gt-exports/diagram-1924-structure-gt.md (confidential, outside repo)
findings.repro: Rebuild diagram 1924 (K=1) via the app; compare drawn structure to owner GT

findings.summary: Rebuilt diagram: son rendered as married to his mother (relation-type flip, partner_of vs child_of). GT detail: btcopilot-sources/training/gt-exports/diagram-1924-structure-gt.md (confidential, outside repo)
findings.repro: Rebuild diagram 1924 (K=1) via the app; compare drawn structure to owner GT

findings.summary: Ex-partner left floating; owner ruling: ex-partners belong on the diagram (criteria change, overturns the legit-disconnect assumption for this class). GT detail: btcopilot-sources/training/gt-exports/diagram-1924-structure-gt.md (confidential, outside repo)
findings.repro: Rebuild diagram 1924 (K=1) via the app; compare drawn structure to owner GT

findings.summary: Committed-data poison on diagram 1924 blocks 5 GT assertions: proband's committed parents point at the wrong couple's bond; one person seated under her sibling's bond via a wrong committed birth event; two spurious committed bonds. Unreachable by the additive delta by design.
findings.repro: Read-only committed audit: /tmp/fd338_evidence/ralph/committed_audit.json; identical failure set across 2 independent rebuilds

findings.summary: apply_parent_edits consumed ALL positive-id rows on full accept, silently destroying pending name/gender Update cards for legacy clients — fixed: only parents-only rows consumed, others stay staged

Parents-edit channel now fires on the regular /extract path and applies server-side on full accept with NO review card (client hides the rows) — a wrong null-to-set parents link can reach the committed diagram silently. Mitigations: only fills when parents is null; conflicts dropped. Design call: should parents edits get review cards?
findings.repro: Multi-window extract staging a positive-id parents row; accept all; row applied without review

findings.summary: OVERNIGHT 2026-06-24 GT extension + named-proband measurement (8 second-walk corrections N1-N8 encoded into diagram-1924-edge-assertions.json, 32 assertions total; confidential names live only in btcopilot-sources/gt-exports). N1 (FD-321 target) CLEARED deterministically: proband duplicate gone, no neutral-placeholder person, 5/5 runs vs the real pre-FD-321 committed snapshot which DID contain the duplicate. N2-N8 NOT cleared in the proxy: N2 wife-is-a-parent fails 5/5 (placeholder fabrication); N4 sister-shares-parents fails 3/5; N5 brother's-wife bond fails 5/5 (merge weld); N7 trio's named mother fails 5/5 (placeholder fabrication) with occasional Elizabeth-misattach forbidden violation (extraction draw); N8 nephew-on-right-uncle fails 5/5 (extraction same-first-name collision). Residual class is merge-weld + placeholder-fabrication, NOT a bounded downstream edit (prior placeholder-spouse reconciliation pass measured, no effect). Material caveat: the proxy is a single from-empty K=1 accumulation with NO committed base and NO merge_runs consensus, so it understates structural quality; the honest C12/C14 verdict requires the real deep_reextract entrypoint against the named committed primary.
findings.repro: From /Users/patrick/worktrees/deep-reextract/btcopilot: source /tmp/fd338_evidence/overnight_env.sh && env -u VIRTUAL_ENV uv run --no-sync python /tmp/fd338_evidence/overnight/rebuild.py <out.json>; then scorer.py <out.json> against gt-exports/diagram-1924-edge-assertions.json

findings.summary: Dock could only weld a floating person onto the main tree; two separate floating fragments could never be joined to each other even when the transcript states the link. Working-tree fix to dock.py _gated: a floating member may anchor to a member of a DIFFERENT floating group (same-group self-anchors still rejected). Covered by test_dock_attaches_across_floating_groups; dock suite 12/12 green. Uncommitted.

deferred_risk.what: Pre-existing extraction-quality bugs on AI-residue test data (F-001 Client-dup, F-002 reject-cursor, stale full_accept race, count-toast mismatch, assistant-bond weld-through)
deferred_risk.why: Patrick: 'we need to defer existing bugs outside the scope of this ticket... we also don't need to be burning a ton of tokens and days on test data that isn't produced by the e2e extraction flow'
deferred_risk.carried_to: follow-up tickets after the human e2e walk

deferred_risk.what: F-006 rebuild-button discoverability (hidden until discussion selected)
deferred_risk.why: Patrick: 'it appeared after selecting a discussion. We can defer that bug for later'

history.note: Ticket ACs accepted as oracle; architecture re-derived from first principles (council + probes) with Patrick's sign-off ('do it')
gate-and-dock built + fix round; suites green; acceptance battery 7/7 >=94.3%

Agent track: C1-C5 verified; C6-C11 pending app-harness run. Human track: C12. First human script REJECTED by Patrick for rule violations (coordinator-authored, agent-track not exhausted, no guards run); redo in progress.

Agent track C6-C11 all verified on the real Personal app (worktree code, bridge port 9899, offscreen QML) against sandbox flask 8889 + real Celery worker, using seeded test user 2308 / diagram 2011 / discussion 61 (scripts + logs in /tmp/fd338_evidence/agent/). Diagram 1924 untouched. New findings F-003 (merge dup-over-committed, major, same class as deferred F-001), F-004 (local pdp residue, minor), F-005 (duplicate overlay-cancel objectName blocks harness-driven cancel, minor).

Human walk on real diagram surfaced 5 structural errors (F-007..F-011); C12 not passed; unit returns to build for in-scope fixes; F-011 is a criteria change awaiting oracle confirmation (ex-partners attach)

Overnight unattended run after FD-321 merge. Extended GT to 32 assertions (8 second-walk corrections N1-N8). Measured FD-321+FD-338 on a NAMED FROM-EMPTY PROXY (5 runs). RESULT: FD-321's proband triplication is killed deterministically (N1 cleared 5/5; the real pre-FD-321 committed snapshot DID contain the duplicate). Broader structural corrections NOT cleared in the proxy (required 5-7/17): proband parents wrong, brother/wife/named-mother absent or placeholder, nephew on wrong uncle. CAVEAT the proxy omits merge_runs consensus against the committed base and understates quality; honest C12/C14 verdict needs the real deep_reextract entrypoint. F1 guardrail green under naming (People 0.92-0.94, Events 0.40-0.46). One working-tree code change: dock cross-floating-group weld (F-016). C12 left pending (human re-walk), C14 left failed (not faked). Git untouched; diagram 1924 primary name set to a real name (reversible, snapshot exists). Report: /tmp/fd338_evidence/overnight/OVERNIGHT_REPORT.md.

parked_on: Conversational-editing loop GREEN-LIT, scope now BIDIRECTIONAL: (1) system asks a clarifying question when the rebuild leaves a gap; (2) user directly corrects the family in chat ("vibe-coding the family" — "Meredith is Connies sister, not daughter") and the coach applies it as a structural edit. Both mutate structure with the user as authority; corrections persist as transcript/GT so every future rebuild inherits them. Separate feature (question ledger + in-chat correction parser/applier + the coach prompt currently handles neither + UI) => its own ticket under epic FD-264. FD-338 rebuild ships now with C12 carried as deferred_risk pointing at it. Awaiting Patrick: explicit yes to draft the ticket.
